questions: Checkpoint cleanup: Should old checkpoints (>24h) be pruned client-side on load, via a Supabase DB trigger on INSERT, or via a scheduled Edge Function? Client-side prune is simplest (DELETE WHERE created_at < now()-24h on each loadCheckpoints call). DB trigger adds server-side guarantee but is less flexible.

jumpToCheckpoint behavior: When jumping to a checkpoint, should the undo stack be (a) cleared entirely, (b) preserved so you can undo back, or (c) should the jump itself be a single undo entry? Option (a) is simplest and most predictable. Option (c) is most flexible but adds complexity.

Should applyResolvedConfigEdit (used by agent chat / AI edits) also participate in undo, or should AI-driven edits be excluded from the undo stack? Currently the agent chat can make large config changes via this path.
success_criteria: Cmd+Z undoes the most recent edit and restores the full previous timeline state (clips, tracks, positions, metadata)
Cmd+Shift+Z redoes after an undo, and redo stack is cleared when a new edit is made after undo
Dragging a clip collapses into a single undo entry (undo restores to pre-drag state, not intermediate positions)
Rapid slider/property edits within 300ms collapse into a single undo entry
Undo/redo buttons appear next to save badge, are disabled when stack is empty, and visually indicate availability
History dropdown shows checkpoint list with labels, trigger types, and timestamps
Checkpoints auto-create on session boundary (>5min idle gap), edit-distance cap (~30 edits), and before destructive operations
Manual 'Save checkpoint' creates a named checkpoint visible in the history panel
Jumping to a checkpoint fully restores the timeline to that saved state
Checkpoints persist across page reloads (stored in Supabase), limited to past 24 hours
Undo triggers a normal save to Supabase (the restored state is persisted)
No performance regression: undo stack snapshots stay under 2MB total, checkpoint saves are async
assumptions: Undo snapshots will store the serializable config (TimelineConfig) rather than the full TimelineData, and rebuild rows/meta/resolved on restore using existing buildDataFromCurrentRegistry(). This avoids stale URLs and keeps snapshots lean.

The onBeforeCommit hook approach (modifying commitData in useTimelineSave.ts) is preferred over wrapping applyTimelineEdit, because it captures ALL mutation paths (applyTimelineEdit, applyResolvedConfigEdit) with a single interception point.
Checkpoint cleanup will be done client-side (DELETE on load) unless you specify otherwise. No Edge Function or DB trigger needed for the initial implementation.
canUndo/canRedo will be React state (boolean) updated only when the stack transitions between empty and non-empty, to avoid re-rendering the entire editor tree on every edit.
The undo stack cap is 100 entries and checkpoint cap is 30 per timeline — both are generous defaults that can be tuned later.
jumpToCheckpoint will clear the undo/redo stacks (option a from the questions), as this is most predictable for users.
Agent chat edits via applyResolvedConfigEdit WILL participate in undo (they flow through commitData like everything else), unless you say otherwise.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
